content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662        322.5660           322.5901          322.6441        322.6444

March 13, 1979

Kirk Alan Pessner
Dobbs & Nielsen
Suite 1500, The Alcoa Bldg.
One Maritime Plaza
San Francisco, CA  94111

Dear Kirk:

     This is in response to your letter dated February 6, 1979, concerning major donor thresholds.  I apologize for the delay in responding.

     Your first question is whether a person which qualified as a major donor committee in 1978 is required to file any campaign or abbreviated statements in 1979 prior to the time it has made campaign contributions of $5,000 and requalified as a major donor committee.  Pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18404(a), a major donor committee terminates at the end of a calendar year.  Therefore, under Government Code Section 82013(c), a major donor committee must requalify each year as a committee.  Until $5,000 in campaign contributions are made, the person does not become a major donor committee for 1979.  However, there will be a requirement under Section 84300, for the maker of an in-kind contribution of $100 or more to provide a written notice to the recipient of the value of the contribution.  This requirement exists for all makers, not just those that are committees.

     Your second question is whether a 1978 major donor committee must file a campaign statement in 1979 if, in 1979, it forgives a loan that it made and reported in 1978. There was not sufficient activity, other than the loan forgiveness, in 1979 to qualify as a major donor.  A loan forgiveness is included within the definition of expenditure. Section 82025.  Therefore, assuming the loan forgiveness to be for an amount of $5,000 or more, by forgiving the loan in 1979, the committee will requalify for 1979 as a major donor committee under Section 82013(c).  The loan forgiveness will of course also have to be disclosed on the recipient committee's next campaign statement.

___________________________________

1/  All statutory references are to the Government Code.

SUPERSEDED
58-460

C2013(C)

Kirk Alan Pessner
March 13, 1979
Page Two


     If the major donor committee has no other activity in
1979, a semi-annual campaign statement should be filed with
each filing officer with whom the last campaign statement
was filed.

     I hope this information has been helpful to you.  Feel
free to call if you have any questions or comments.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara Campbell
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

BC:kp

DOBBS & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law
SUITE 2500, THE ALCOA BUILDING
ONE MARITIME PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111

TELEPHONE (415) 362-1940

February 6, 1979


Ms. Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95807

Re: Major Donor Threshold

Dear Gina:

     I would appreciate your written response to
the following questions:

     1. Is it true that a major donor filer in
1978, need not file any major donor or abbreviated
statements in 1979 until major donor status is reached
even if over $250 is given to a local candidate prior
to reaching $5,000.

     2. If a major donor in 1978 made a loan to
a campaign committee in that year, but forgives that
loan in 1979, while not having yet reached major donor
status for 1979, are they required to file major donor
statements for 1979 as a result of the forgiveness of
the loan?

     Inasmuch as the loan was used in 1978 to reach
major donor status and the forgiveness of the loan does
not affect the cumulative totals for the previous or
present year nor, according to the advice you gave me
last week, does it count toward the $5,000 threshold in
1979, and the campaign committee to whom the loan was
forgiven would be reporting it as such on its next
campaign filing, there seems to be no reason for the
major donor to file beyond the end of the calendar year
1978. But if the commission requires a report in 1979
because of this forgiveness, when no other report is
required, when should it be filed and who are the filing
officers?


SACRAMENTO OFFICE · SUITE 550, 1225 EIGHTH STREET, SACRAMENTO, CA 95814  •  (916) 446-6752

Ms. Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Page Two
February 6, 1979


                    Thank you for your prompt attention to this
matter.     I look forward to your response.

                                        Kind regards,


                                        [signature]

                                        Kirk Alan Pessner
                                        Paralegal

KAP:la

sections.question: 1. Is it true that a major donor filer in 1978, need not file any major donor or abbreviated statements in 1979 until major donor status is reached even if over $250 is given to a local candidate prior to reaching $5,000. 2. If a major donor in 1978 made a loan to a campaign committee in that year, but forgives that loan in 1979, while not having yet reached major donor status for 1979, are they required to file major donor statements for 1979 as a result of the forgiveness of the loan?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether a 1978 major donor must file statements in 1979 before reaching the $5,000 threshold, and whether forgiving a loan made in 1978 triggers major donor filing requirements in 1979 even if the forgiveness does not count toward the current year's threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. Document contains two specific numbered questions about major donor threshold and loan forgiveness treatment. Document is partially cut off at the bottom of page two.

embedding.summary: An attorney at Dobbs & Nielsen seeks written FPPC guidance on major donor filing requirements for 1979, specifically regarding threshold triggers and the treatment of loan forgiveness from prior-year contributions.
